{{Quote|Time is on our side|Motto of the Republic of Level 153}}
__NOTOC__
==Description==
'''The Republic of Level 153''' is a group based in [[Level 153]]. The group has a population of around 10,000<ref>Counting all citizens of the Republic, both those living in an outpost of the Republic and those alone or residing with other groups</ref>, the majority of which reside in Level 153, although they have created outposts in other levels. The group declares itself an independent state, having its own system of government, education system, and bureaucracy. While not having much influence outside its titular level, it is the dominant force within Level 153, and exerts limited authority over other outposts within the level. The Republic is also occasionally called the '''The Republic of the Antique Workspace'''<ref>Formerly The Republic of the Modern Workspace, the level's nickname before the 21st century.</ref>, after the level's name chosen before the current level numbering system, leading to citizens of the group to be nicknamed '''Workspacers'''.

Like its allies the [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists]], the Republic of Level 153 has individual freedom as one of its core values. This allows the average resident of the group to choose their own role in the group's everyday functioning rather than having it be assigned by the government. This mainly applies to those in the group's main bases within Level 153, as in their smaller outposts more direct control is needed to ensure their survival.  

The Republic is typically neutral to individual wanderers, welcoming only those they deem trustworthy into the group, although there is some xenophobic sentiment among a minority of their members. After spending at least 5 years within the group, one can request to gain citizenship. Citizenship can be revoked from those who join or assist rival groups or commit offenses against the Republic. The children of citizens of the group also gain citizenship<ref>This formerly only applied to those born within Level 153</ref>. All citizens over the age of 21 are considered legal adults and can be appointed to a government position or run for president.
==Government and Leadership==
The government of '''The Republic of Level 153''' based on a combination of principles of a hybrid of representative and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Direct_democracy direct democracy] as well as [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classical_liberalism classical liberalism], with voters directly electing a president every 3 years, with elections occurring on August 1st. The president has the power to make official decisions and acts within the bounds of the Republic's constitution. The populace of the group can call a vote to veto any decision, with 1/4 of the population dissenting to call a veto vote, and 2/3rds to enact the veto . Voting is mandatory for elections, veto decisions, or any other form of voting, with all votes being conducted at 12 PM, Level 153 Standard Time <ref>A time zone created by the residents of Level 153 to account for the time dilation within their level.</ref>.

The president of the Republic appoints various members of the group to managerial positions, having authority over various parts of the group's affairs and management. Current managerial roles are those for Trade, Expansion, Health, Security, Labor, Justice, and Education. These managers additionally nominate several members of the group to assist them. Decisions by these managers can be vetoed in a manner identical to those of the president. The president has the power to remove one from a managerial role or replace them with another at any time.

A president can be removed from office through a popular vote. To call this impeachment vote requires 1/4 of the population's support, and to convict the president requires 3/4ths approval. To this date, only one president, Marco Albertson<ref>Elected in 1997 and convicted in 1999</ref>,who was found embezzling the group's supplies for personal gain, was convicted with 84% of the vote, although proceedings have been enacted several other times.

Each individual outpost of the Republic has its own governor, who is elected by the residents of that outpost. Governors have powers akin to those of the president, although limited to their outpost. Like other government officials, the governors can be impeached through a 3/4ths majority vote, and additionally can be removed by the president, although this decision can be vetoed.

The current president of the Republic of Level 153 is Naomi Reed, who was first elected in 2014 with 38% of the vote as a member of the politically moderate Unity Party. Due to her leadership of the group during [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], she has won re-election in 2016, 2019, and 2023. Reed supports expansionist policy, such as the creation of more outposts outside Level 153 and alliances with other groups. Reed has announced that she will not run for re-election in 2026, and instead endorses a former governor of the Secondary Base, Luis Omarov.  
==History==
===Discovery and Founding===
Level 153 was first discovered in 1956 by the M.E.G.<ref>Level 153's date of discovery seems to follow a pattern of levels set in particular time periods being discovered about 30 years before that time period, although there are some exceptions, such as [[Level 15]]</ref> Settlement of the level began in the 1960s, taking advantage of the ready food supply and relatively little danger, and by 1969 it boasted a population of 354.

In the early 1970s, the renovation of [[Level 154]] by [[Architect Industries]] was underway, and by 1974, Hermes Drive-powered trains were travelling between many levels of The Backrooms, including Level 153. Due to Level 153 both being accessible from and having an entrance to Level 154, it began to greatly profit off of the influx of resources and wanderers entering the levels. This caused Level 153's population to soar to 2,000 by the end of the decade. Many of the new inhabitants of the level were not affiliated with any group, and thus banded together to form their own small groups or joined one of the pre-existing settlements within the level. 

In 1982, the various settlements within Level 153 not belonging to an outside group decided to unify into one group. Representatives of each group worked to draft a constitution defining the processes of its government, as well as granting personal freedoms, inspired by the liberal democracies of The Frontrooms. The constitution was completed and accepted on April 2nd, 1982 The [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists]], who shared similar values and had an outpost in the level that time, supported the group's formation and was one of the first to have diplomatic relations with it.
=== Early Republic===
The Republic held its first elections on August 1st of that year. Ryan Moreau, one of the key individuals who founded the group, won with 90.3% of the vote, and was re-elected in the 1985 and 1989 elections before resigning in 1990.

Under its new leadership, the Republic of Level 153 flourished in its early years. During the remainder of the 1980s, the group mainly focused on centralization, creating a central base where the government would reside, in addition to creating new outposts to take advantage of newly discovered Break Rooms within the level in order to feed its growing population.

In 1992, an event known as the Two Week Year took place. As the name implies, a sudden time shift within the level caused what felt like 2 weeks in Level 153 to be 10 months elsewhere. A consequence of this event was that the Level 153 Time would be 1 year ahead of Frontrooms Standard Time, although this was later corrected by several smaller distortions over the following years.

During the 1990s, the Republic gradually grew in influence over its home level, with various smaller groups and outposts such as the 153 Wanderer's Association, the Librarians, and the Clock Watchers being absorbed into it or otherwise aligning themselves with it. By 1998, the majority of the level's population, not counting those belonging to groups based outside the level, was under the jurisdiction of the Republic.
===Era of Expansion===
At the turn of the century, the Republic of Level 153 focused its interests outside the level. The Republic established a base in [[Level 1]] in 2002 and one in [[Level 4]] in 2003, and by 2010 had a presence in [[Level 427]], [[Level 246]], [[Level 6.1]], and [[Level 20]], as well as minor outposts in a myriad of other levels.

This expansion did not come without difficulties. In July of 2005, due to a territorial dispute, the Republic's base in Level 4 was attacked by the Abandoned Office United Front, a local group with a population of approximately 1,000, marking the Republic's first conflict outside of Level 153. The A.O.U.F greatly outnumbered the Workspacer base, allowing them to quickly overwhelm and occupy it. However, several days later, the Republic sent in reinforcements to attempt to retake the base, succeeding, and then launching an attack on the main outpost of the A.O.U.F. The A.O.U.F subsequently surrendered, recognizing the Republic's claim on their outpost<ref>The A.O.U.F began to decline in the 2010s, and was reported as being fully destroyed in [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]].</ref>. Total casualties in the conflict numbered at around 30-40.

Meanwhile, within Level 153, the Republic was expanding inward, replacing the level's now antique and occasionally faulty technology with modern computers and [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] terminals, as well as greatly changing and improving the structure of the surroundings of their major bases within the level, such as modifying the fire sprinklers to release a smaller quantity of water. Artificial heating was additionally installed in their main base, which greatly aided the group during [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. These renovations were accomplished with the help of [[Architect Industries]].

During the height of the Korthie Casiagen War, volunteers from the Republic supported [[Republic of Aurielle|Auriellen]] forces, providing them with supplies and occasionally sent volunteers to join in the fighting, using equipment provided by the [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists]].  

In 2014, Naomi Reed, the current president of the Republic was elected with a narrow majority. While she did not cause monumental change during her first term, and may otherwise have been destined for the trash bin of history, she proved to be the one of the group's greatest leaders. 
===The Flicker===
February 2nd, 2016 began as an ordinary day for the Republic.  The citizens of the group were going about their normal lives, doing their jobs, and time was flowing at a steady 1.03 of baseline. No one could predict [[Phenomenon 12|the catastrophe]] that was to befall the entire dimension<ref>Although data from Kauer experiments shows an uptick in nano-scale quantum activity in the level in the week preceding the onset of the Flicker, it is unknown if it is a coincidence or directly related.</ref>.  At precisely 2:21 PM, the level's lights abruptly ceased to function. For unknown reasons, possibly due to the level's already unstable space-time receiving a shock, the initial onset of The Flicker caused an abrupt Time Stop, lasting almost 2 days Frontrooms Time.

When the inhabitants of the Level regained their senses, what they found was not a pretty sight. The level was put into pitch darkness, and all its electronics. Before the level's inhabitants had time to make sense of what was going on, they realized they had a bigger problem on their hands. Due to the sprinkler systems that ordinarily put out the fires that occasionally start in :Level 153 no longer being functional due to the power loss, fires began to consume many parts of the level, forcing the Workspacers to put them out manually, but not before fires consumed a major storage site of the Republic, burning up crucial resources.

3 weeks later, the wanderers of Level 153 found an even worse problem: rather than the level being too hot, it became too cold. Temperatures plummeted to -8 Celsius, resulting in many wanderers getting frostbite and even hypothermia, and having to burn their possessions for warmth. President Reed, who had already been struggling to keep up with managing the group, was faced with a difficult choice. They would either have to leave the level, and potentially risk going to an even more dangerous location and leaving part of the group behind, or stay, which every day seemed to be a more risky choice. After hours of agonizing and consulting with managers, Reed made a choice: the Republic would evacuate into [[Level 154]].

After entering Level 154, the group soon encountered the remnants of the Confederation of Level 154, which had previously been their rival due to their competing government systems and values. Despite having an initial standoffish attitude, the diplomats of the two groups soon reached an agreement: the Confederation would share their ample food stores, while the Republic would lend their weapons so that the groups could fight off a number of entities entering from [[Level 154.1]]. After another 2 months of hardship , the lights came on simultaneously in levels 153 and 154 on May 3rd, 2016, at 5:19 AM. Due to this, May 3rd is considered a national holiday in Level 153, a day of remembrance for the approximately 5,000 Workspacers around the Backrooms who perished during the Flicker.
===Recovery===
After the Flicker ceased, the surviving population of the group in Level 154 moved back into their bases within their home level, finding that a large portion of their supplies were looted or rendered unusable. There, they reunited with some of the isolated bases that had remained within the level. Many of the residents of these bases were suffering from cold-related ailments and starvation, and had almost no supplies remaining, with some outposts being found completely empty, their populations presumably perishing.

With the help of their allies, the two sections of the Republic reunited, and began the process of recovering from the  catastrophe. Due to most of their infrastructure within Level 153 being intact, recovery went quickly, with many of the Republic's larger bases becoming repopulated. Upon reaching some form of stability, the Republic helped send aid and supplies to areas of the Backrooms still recovering, in addition to some of their citizens joining relief efforts.

Currently, the Republic is once again entering a period of expansionism reminiscent to that in the beginning of the 2000s, setting up new outposts across the Backrooms and entering the complex world of inter-group politics.
==Culture and Politics==
The official language of the Republic of Level 153 is English, although members of the group know a variety other Frontrooms languages, as well as Neon Creole<ref>A creole language popular in the "neon cluster" of levels accessible from [[Level 154]], consisting of a combination of English, Spanish, Mandarin Chinese, [[Aurielleno|Aurelleno]], Arabic, and Korean.</ref>. Members of the Republic, especially those residing in Level 153 additionally speak with a noticeable accent that has developed over time.

Ethnically, the population of the Republic is varied. The initial wanderers who founded the group mainly came from Western Europe and the United States, and based the Republic's system of governance off the liberal democracies in those areas. With the rapid growth of the Republic's population, wanderers originating from a myriad of different locations on Terra joined the group, making its population one of the most ethnically heterogeneous for a group originating on a single level. Additionally, a growing share of the Republic's population is Backborn<ref>A term for humans born in The Backrooms</ref>, and is set to overtake that of Frontrooms-born wanderers by 2030. 

The Republic's population almost entirely consists of humans. The official policy of the group is a moderate anti-entity stance, believing hostile entities should be exterminated, but that sapient entities should be treated as people. However, the Republic holds the belief that different types of sapient beings cannot coexist in societies together, and do not allow nonhumans to become citizens, although they do allow them to seek refuge temporarily at outposts of the group for short periods of time.  

The Workspacer community has over time developed various rumors and urban legends, particularly about the anomalous properties of Level 153. The most famous of these is that of The Anomaly, a hypothetical entity responsible for moving around items during the level's Time Stop events. Other explanations include a theory that large alterations in the level's space-time such as the Two Week Year are caused by secret experiments carried out by the local base of [[The Kauer Research Organization]]. The K.R.O denies this claim.

The current political situation in the Republic is tense, with multiple different factions attempting to fill the void left by President Reed in the 2026 election<ref>The 2026 Election, despite officially occurring on 1 August, 2026, will likely take place in March of 2027, Frontrooms Time, due to Level 153's time dilation.</ref>. The Unity Party, a liberal centrist political party that Reed belonged to has close competition with its main rival, the National Liberal Party, having last held office in 2011. Challenging the two mainstream parties is the rising Solidarity Party, a right-wing populist party. The Solidarity Party has been endorsed by Backborn nationalist groups on BACK-NET, drawing controversy. The party responded to this by rejecting the endorsements and condemning the groups, although a minority of its voters have been known to show support to them.

==Economy and Capabilities==

'''The Republic of Level 153''''s economy is mainly decentralized, with most trade being managed by individual members of the group. When conducting large inter-group trade deals, the Republic's trade manager has the authority to select some of these members to act as representatives of the group during the deal, with others volunteering to do this full-time. 

There is no official currency made by the Republic, with them instead accepting currencies of various other groups, such as liters of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], the [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonist]] Mina, and the [[Republic of Aurielle|Auriellen]] Nolic. In 1997, an official currency, the Workspace Dollar, was established, but was abolished in 1999. Several attempts at creating an official currency have been attempted since, the most recent being in 2011, gaining support from nationalist and isolationist Workspacers, but have all been voted against. 

The Republic conducts trade through the easily accessible [[Level 154]]. The group imports weapons, manufactured goods, medicine, and electronics from other levels, while exporting food and computer parts. One notable export of the Republic is knowledge. Books and journals within Level 153's Library of Time contain valuable information about certain parts of the Backrooms. The Republic trades these books to the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]], the M.E.G, and the [[Backroom Colonists]] for their [[The Database|Database]] directories<ref>Due to the tendency of the books to occasionally contain incorrect information, they are checked against current sources about the level before the trade is conducted.</ref>, sparing them the difficulty of entering dangerous levels to find information. 

The Republic has access to a sizable store of resources, most notably [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and some of its [[Extended Almond Water Sub-Item Dossier (Collaborative)|Sub-Items]] due to its ready availability in Level 153. In addition to Almond Water, the group  possesses lesser stores of [[Object 27|Mixologist's Delight]], [[Object 49|Wallpaper Marshmallows]], [[Object 19|Portable Purifiers]], edible Backshrooms, and [[Object 34|Spices of Life]]. 

Additionally, the group grows various edible plants through hydroponics. This was originally done to benefit the health of the group's populace, as while food is abundant in Level 153, many of it is heavily processed and unhealthy, although the consumption of [[Extended Almond Water Sub-Item Dossier (Collaborative)|Almond Water-based foods]] partially counteracted this. Plants grown through hydroponics in Level 153 include strawberries, lettuce, tomatoes, and marijuana. Despite this, the Republic still needs to import plant-based food as well as unprocessed meats from other levels to meet their growing population's demands.

In terms of military capabilities, the Republic is mostly focused on defense. The most well-defended outposts controlled by the group are those in Level 153, with those in other levels mainly having standard guard measures to defend against entities and raiders. Bases in Level 153, especially the group's central base are heavily guarded. Guards in the level are equipped with imported military-grade equipment in addition to ordinary weaponry found commonly found in the backrooms, and are trained to utilize the level's distorted time and layout to their advantage. While the group mostly uses conventional weaponry, they additionally have stockpiles of [[Object 29|Kalthoff Rifles]], [[Object 15|Firesalt]], [[Object 5|Greek Fire]], and various [[Object 10|Backshrooms]].<ref>Incendiary weapons are rendered less effective in Level 153 due to the level's highly reactive automatic sprinklers, making their use less practical for the Republic.</ref> The high level of protection, in addition to the nature of Level 153 inhibiting the use of most military vehicles, makes a direct invasion of the level difficult even for large and well-equipped groups.

With the collaboration of the [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists]] and the Level 153 base of the [[The Kauer Research Organization]], the Republic has also gained access to their Entrance/Exit manipulation technology. While these devices, which are used to teleport between levels are still highly experimental and prone to failure, the unstable and malleable space-time of Level 153<ref>7.8 on the [[Phenomenon 11|Morrison Stability Scale]]</ref> makes the level an ideal testing ground, with the devices having a success rate there of over 92%, greater even than that of [[Level 0]].

Through combining components of these devices with those of the computers in Level 153, engineers from the Republic have developed a new form of experimental device, the Time Stop device. Like its name implies, these devices create a sphere of stopped time 1.5 meters in diameter in front of them. Within these spheres time is completely still<ref>This results in the spheres being completely black in color, as light also stops when coming in contact with the sphere.</ref>, similar to time during Level 153's Time Stop event, lasting for 30 seconds before these spheres dissipate. These can be used for a variety of purposes, such as defense from entity attacks or simply holding objects in place. However, the Time Stop devices rely on Level 153's distorted space-time to function, and do not function properly outside Level 153 or similarly unstable levels<ref>Levels scoring a 7.3 or greater on the [[Phenomenon 11|Morrison Stability Scale]]</ref>. It is rumored that the Republic has installed some Time Stop devices in the walls of the level, positioned in a way that would allow them to create a "sheet" of stopped time when activating, blocking the hallway, presumably to help protect the level in case of invasion.      

==Major Bases and Settlements==
*Central Base- [[Level 153]]
**Population: aprox. 5,000
**The group's main base, located within Level 153, and the seat of their government.
*Secondary Base- [[Level 153]]
**Population: aprox. 1,500
**A base built by The Republic around 100 kilometers northwest of their main base, and serves as their main source of farmed food.
*Storage Base- [[Level 153]]
**Population: aprox. 500
**A base built to take advantage of a cluster of 5 nearby Break Rooms, and is focused on supply storage.
*Far Base- [[Level 153]]
**Population: approx. 300
**A base built 1,200 kilometers away from the Central Base in order to advance exploration into new parts of the level and potentially secure new supplies.
*Outpost First Step- [[Level 1]]
**Population: approx. 800
**The first outpost outside Level 153 built after [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], built at the site of a previous Republic base destroyed during the event.
*Outpost Gray Office- [[Level 4]]
**Population: approx. 150
**Built near an entrance to Level 153.
*Outpost Neon Gate- [[Level 154]]
**Population: approx. 550
**A base built to house trade representatives who wish to reside in Level 154.
*Outpost Felt Kitchen-  [[Level 86]]
**Population: approx. 100
**Exports food to other outposts of The Republic.
*Outpost Verdant Shelter- [[Level 427]]
**Population: aprox. 350
**The only outpost of the group outside of Level 153 to have survived the Flicker.
